events.note: Writing Intent Check (revision 1) for the ECMP false-positive task

events.change_summary: Intent Check (revision 1): problem framing, expected outcomes, and scope boundaries for cutting ECMP-driven High Jitter false positives

events.summary: Intent Check pushed as revision 1. Covers all three levers from the brief: larger probe samples in the fallback, a Paris-capable prober when available, and preferring nearby Globalping measurements for the loss/jitter verdict. Approve to proceed to the full scope, or reply with refinements.

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) after approved intent check

events.change_summary: Full scope: 11 requirements across fallback sampling, Paris-capable probing, and near-target verdict gating; 8 acceptance criteria; 3 implementation phases with files and verification

events.rationale: Fixes existing broken behavior (false High Jitter verdicts) with no UI beyond existing terminal tables — skip design. Work follows established single-purpose module conventions (new paris.py mirrors pmtu.py/latency.py, diagnosis gating mirrors the rate-limited-hop pattern) and touches ~6 files across trace, diagnosis, and remote-measurement layers without new system boundaries — skip architecture; detailed phases with Files and Verification carry the design.

events.decision: All three levers from the brief are in scope as separate phases: raise the fallback sample count, add best-effort Paris-capable prober support, and gate country-sweep verdicts on Globalping near-target loss/jitter.
events.rationale: The levers address independent failure modes — sample-size quantization, ECMP flow divergence, and vantage-point bias — and each is independently shippable. Paris probers need the same raw-socket privileges that already force mtr into the fallback on unprivileged macOS, so phase 2 is strictly best-effort (silent fall-through to system traceroute) and phases 1 and 3 must eliminate the false positives on their own.

events.decision: Remote-figure verdict gating lives inside the pure diagnose() function via new keys on the result dict (ping_loss_pct, ping_jitter_ms in the existing per-row globalping dict); country mode re-runs diagnose() on rows that gained remote data after the Globalping merge, before the summary table and exit-code computation. The jitter threshold (JITTER_WARNING_MS = 10.0) is unchanged.
events.rationale: Follows the established project rule that context-dependent signal re-classification belongs in diagnosis.py (the pure-function module), not in transport modules or cli.py. Re-diagnosis is required because Globalping results merge after diagnose() already ran inside _measure(); without it the summary verdicts and exit code would ignore the near-target data. Keeping the threshold fixed keeps the change about input quality, not calibration drift.

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2: 11 requirements, 8 acceptance criteria, 3 phases. Phase 1 fixes the fallback prober to honor the requested probe count (the probes parameter is currently ignored and -q 2 is hardcoded) and gates the High Jitter check on sample size. Phase 2 adds a best-effort paris.py module (dublin-traceroute, then scamper) into the fallback chain with silent fall-through. Phase 3 raises Globalping ping to 16 packets, parses near-target loss/jitter, and re-derives country-sweep verdicts after the remote merge so the summary and exit code reflect near-target quality. Workflow: bug-fix / standard, no design or architecture stage.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting phase 1: meaningful samples in the fallback prober

events.description: Starting phase 1: Meaningful samples in the fallback prober

events.details: Baseline was 103 passed. Added 6 tests: 3 in tests/test_mtr.py (probe-count threading, cap at 5, timeout scaling) and 3 in tests/test_diagnosis.py (jitter suppressed at probe_count=2 with informational signal, warning fires at probe_count=5 and 10). Updated 2 exact-call-signature assertions in tests/test_country.py for the new probes kwarg. ruff check src tests: all checks passed.

events.decision: Implementation: the sample-size jitter gate applies only when probe_count is present and below 5; probe_count=None keeps the legacy High Jitter behavior, and the informational jitter_low_sample signal is emitted only when jitter actually exceeds the 10 ms threshold.
events.rationale: diagnose() receives arbitrary dicts (tests, asn mode) where probe_count may be absent; treating None as "unknown" rather than "too few" preserves every existing test and the mtr path (cycles=10) unchanged. Emitting the ok-severity note only above the threshold keeps Healthy runs free of noise. The effective fallback probe count is recorded in cli.py via min(cycles, mtr.TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES) in one shared spot after the trace section, covering all three trace branches (compare_v6, ecmp, plain).

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Meaningful samples in the fallback prober

events.summary: Fallback prober now honors the requested per-hop probe count (capped at TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES=5, timeout scaled to the count), _measure() records the effective probe_count when the traceroute fallback ran, and diagnose() suppresses the High Jitter warning below 5 samples in favor of an ok-severity jitter_low_sample informational signal. 6 new tests; 109 passed; ruff clean.

items.description: On this machine (where mtr lacks raw-socket permission), run `netpath asn AS15169` and confirm the terminal still shows the `mtr unavailable — using traceroute` note and the run completes.
While that run's trace is in flight, run `ps aux | grep traceroute` in another terminal and confirm the command line contains `-q 5` (default `--cycles 10` capped to 5) instead of the old `-q 2`.
Run `netpath asn AS15169 --cycles 3` and confirm via `ps aux | grep traceroute` that the command contains `-q 3` — the requested count is threaded through when below the cap.
Run `netpath asn AS15169 --cycles 3 --json` and confirm the JSON output's `probe_count` field is `3` (the effective fallback count), not the raw cycles value when cycles exceed 5.

Pick a long-haul target that previously produced a false High Jitter verdict (e.g. `netpath asn AS9829 --cycles 3`) and confirm the verdict is no longer `High Jitter`; the verdict panel should instead show an informational note that jitter was measured from too few samples.

On a machine where mtr works with raw sockets (or via `sudo`), run `netpath asn AS15169` and confirm behavior is unchanged: 10-cycle mtr, and a jitter above 10 ms still yields the `High Jitter` warning.

events.note: Starting phase 2: consistent-route probing via a Paris-capable prober

events.description: Starting phase 2: Consistent-route probing via a Paris-capable prober

events.details: Baseline was 109 passed. Added 20 tests in tests/test_paris.py: binary detection order (dublin-traceroute preferred, scamper fallback, None when absent), dublin-traceroute parser (per-TTL aggregation across runs, fractional loss, unresponsive hops, empty-payload error), scamper parser (reply grouping, gap-TTL fill, fractional loss, missing-trace error), run() behavior (probe cap at 5, Paris command construction, permission/OS errors mapped to ParisError), and the cli._trace fallback chain (mtr -> paris -> traceroute). ruff check src tests: all checks passed.

alternatives_considered: Single dublin-traceroute invocation with --npaths=probes, aggregating per TTL across flows — rejected because cross-flow aggregation mixes ECMP paths again
A generic "paris" _trace_method value plus a separate _paris_binary result key — rejected as it grows the TypedDict for display-only state
events.decision: Implementation: dublin-traceroute is invoked once per probe with --npaths=1 and the runs aggregated per TTL, rather than one invocation with N paths; _trace_method carries the Paris binary name ("dublin-traceroute" / "scamper") instead of a generic "paris" value.
events.rationale: dublin-traceroute's npaths parameter enumerates N distinct flows, and each flow deliberately takes a different ECMP path — aggregating across flows would reintroduce exactly the path diversity Paris probing is meant to remove. Running the binary probes times with a single pinned flow (--npaths=1) yields N samples per TTL that all follow the same path. scamper needs no such loop because trace -q N -Q sends all N attempts per hop on one pinned icmp-paris flow. Storing the binary name in _trace_method lets _run_test() name the prober in the terminal note (R6) and lets _measure() apply the probe-count correction via membership in paris.SUPPORTED_BINARIES, without adding a new result key to the MeasurementResult TypedDict.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Consistent-route probing via a Paris-capable prober

events.summary: New src/netpath/paris.py module adds best-effort Paris traceroute support: detects dublin-traceroute (preferred) or scamper, runs route-pinned probes (dublin: one --npaths=1 run per probe aggregated per TTL; scamper: trace -P icmp-paris -q N -Q), and parses output into Hub-shaped hop dicts. Any failure raises ParisError and falls through silently. cli.py gains a _fallback_trace() helper implementing the mtr -> paris -> traceroute chain (used by both _trace() and the ECMP branch), corrects probe_count for Paris-sourced traces, and names the prober in the terminal note. 20 new tests; 129 passed; ruff clean.

items.description: With no Paris prober installed (confirm with `which dublin-traceroute scamper`), run `netpath asn AS15169` and verify behavior is unchanged: the note still reads `mtr unavailable — using traceroute + Cymru ASN lookup` and the run completes.

Install a Paris prober with `brew install dublin-traceroute` (or `brew install scamper`), run `sudo netpath asn AS15169`... skip sudo; instead run `netpath asn AS15169` and check the note: if the prober has raw-socket access the note reads `mtr unavailable — using dublin-traceroute Paris traceroute + Cymru ASN lookup`; if it lacks privileges the run silently falls back to the plain traceroute note.

While a Paris-probed run is in flight, run `ps aux | grep dublin-traceroute` in another terminal and confirm the command line contains `--npaths=1` (single pinned flow) and that it is invoked repeatedly (once per probe).

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --cycles 10 --json` on a machine where the Paris prober engages and confirm the JSON `probe_count` field is `5` (capped) and `_trace_method` is absent from the JSON while the hop list carries `Loss%`, `Avg`, and `StDev` per hop.

Simulate a broken Paris binary: create an executable that exits 1, e.g. `printf '#!/bin/sh\nexit 1\n' > /tmp/dublin-traceroute && chmod +x /tmp/dublin-traceroute`, then run `PATH=/tmp:$PATH netpath asn AS15169` and confirm the run silently falls through to the system traceroute with no error output.
On a machine where mtr works with raw sockets, run `netpath asn AS15169` and confirm the Paris chain never engages: normal mtr output, no prober note.

events.phase_title: Consistent-route probing via a Paris-capable prober

items.description: With no Paris prober installed (confirm with `which dublin-traceroute scamper`), run `netpath asn AS15169` and verify behavior is unchanged: the note still reads `mtr unavailable — using traceroute + Cymru ASN lookup` and the run completes.

Install a Paris prober with `brew install dublin-traceroute` (or `brew install scamper`), then run `netpath asn AS15169`. If the prober has raw-socket access, the note reads `mtr unavailable — using dublin-traceroute Paris traceroute + Cymru ASN lookup`; if it lacks privileges, the run silently falls back to the plain `using traceroute` note with no error output.

While a Paris-probed run is in flight, run `ps aux | grep dublin-traceroute` in another terminal and confirm the command line contains `--npaths=1` (single pinned flow) and that the binary is invoked repeatedly, once per probe.

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --cycles 10 --json` on a machine where the Paris prober engages and confirm `probe_count` in the JSON is `5` (capped at `PARIS_MAX_PROBES`), with `Loss%`, `Avg`, and `StDev` present on each hop.

Simulate a broken Paris binary: run `printf '#!/bin/sh\nexit 1\n' > /tmp/dublin-traceroute && chmod +x /tmp/dublin-traceroute`, then `PATH=/tmp:$PATH netpath asn AS15169`, and confirm the run silently falls through to the system traceroute with no error output.
On a machine where mtr works with raw sockets, run `netpath asn AS15169` and confirm the Paris chain never engages: normal 10-cycle mtr output and no prober note.

events.note: Starting phase 3: near-target measurements drive country-sweep verdicts

events.description: Starting phase 3: Near-target measurements drive country-sweep verdicts

events.details: Baseline was 129 passed. Added 12 tests: 6 in tests/test_globalping.py (16-packet scheduling payload; parse_ping_stats loss/jitter extraction, median jitter aggregation across probes, loss aggregation from drop/total counts, received-timing packet count, None on unusable data) and 6 in tests/test_diagnosis.py (clean remote figures suppress local High Jitter with an ok-severity note, remote jitter above threshold fires citing the near-target value, near-target loss warning above the calibrated threshold, suppression does not hide remote loss, below-min-packet remote sample falls back to local behavior, absent/malformed globalping data keeps local behavior). ruff check src tests: all checks passed.

alternatives_considered: Enforcing the 5-packet minimum in cli.py by only merging figures when the sample is large enough — rejected because diagnose() must already read the keys defensively, and splitting the policy across two modules makes the pure function's behavior untestable in isolation
A dedicated remote-loss threshold constant — rejected in favor of the existing calibrated tiers the scope explicitly points to
events.decision: Implementation: the minimum-sample gate for near-target figures lives in diagnose() as REMOTE_MIN_PACKETS = 5, keyed on a new ping_packets value (count of received timings) that parse_ping_stats() reports alongside loss_pct and jitter_ms; the near-target loss check reuses the existing calibrated loss-threshold tiers applied to the remote packet count; country-mode re-diagnosis is a small inline loop gated on rows that have a verdict and at least one remote figure.
events.rationale: Putting the gate inside the pure diagnose() function keeps the "at least 5 packets" edge case testable without I/O and means cli.py merges whatever parsed without policy logic. Received timings (not sent packets) is the honest sample size for a jitter StDev. Reusing the LOSS_THRESHOLD_FEW/DEFAULT/MANY tiers on the remote count (16 packets x 3 probes lands in the 20-99 tier when all arrive) avoids inventing a second calibration scheme. Gating re-diagnosis on the presence of a verdict key preserves the existing exit-code contract for skipped ASNs, and gating on remote figures makes the loop a no-op for uncovered ASNs (R11).

events.change_summary: Phase 3 complete: Near-target measurements drive country-sweep verdicts

events.summary: Country sweeps now judge each ISP by near-target Globalping figures when a sufficient sample exists. Globalping ping requests 16 packets and the new parse_ping_stats() extracts loss (summed drop/total), jitter (median of per-probe timing StDevs), and the received-packet count. diagnose() reads the figures defensively: a valid remote sample (>= REMOTE_MIN_PACKETS = 5 packets) drives the High Jitter check — suppressing a local long-haul warning with an ok-severity near-target note, or firing the warning citing the remote value — and a new independent Near-target Packet Loss warning fires above the calibrated loss tiers. Country mode re-runs diagnose() on rows that gained remote figures before the summary and exit code; the summary subrow shows near-target loss/jitter and the remote-sourced verdict. 12 new tests; 141 passed; ruff clean.

items.description: Run `netpath country IN --top 3` from this US host and confirm the run completes, schedules Globalping measurements, and the summary's `[Globalping]` subrows now show `loss X%` and `jitter Y ms` next to the RTT for covered ASNs.

In the same run, confirm ISPs whose near-target jitter is clean no longer carry a `High Jitter` verdict in the summary: the subrow should read `verdict Healthy (near-target)` even where the per-ISP panel printed earlier in the sweep showed High Jitter from the local trace.
Confirm the exit code reflects the post-merge verdicts: after the run, `echo $?` should be `0` when all near-target-covered ISPs resolved to Healthy and no other warnings fired.
Run `netpath country IN --top 3 --no-remote` and confirm verdicts behave exactly as after phases 1 and 2 (local-trace behavior, no `[Globalping]` subrows, no crash in the summary).

While a country run is scheduling, note one printed measurement ID and fetch it with `curl -s https://api.globalping.io/v1/measurements/{id} | head -c 400` — confirm the ping request carried 16 packets.

Pick an ASN with no Globalping coverage in the run output (marked `no Globalping coverage`) and confirm its summary row and verdict are unchanged from the local-trace view — no near-target labels appear for it.

events.summary: All phases approved — implementation ready for review.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.details: All 141 tests passed across test_mtr.py (3 new probe-count tests), test_paris.py (20 new Paris-prober tests), test_globalping.py (6 new parse_ping_stats tests), test_diagnosis.py (9 new jitter/remote-figure tests), and the existing suites. ruff check src tests: All checks passed.

events.change_summary: APPROVE: All 8 acceptance criteria pass. The three-phase fix correctly addresses the false High Jitter verdict root causes: probe count is honored and capped, the verdict engine gates on sample size, the mtr→paris→traceroute chain falls through silently, and country sweeps re-derive verdicts from near-target Globalping figures. 141 tests pass, ruff clean.

events.rationale: 8/8 acceptance criteria passed, 2/2 automated checks passed (141 tests, ruff clean). All three phases verified: probe count threading and cap in mtr.py, Paris prober module with silent fall-through in paris.py, near-target verdict gating in diagnosis.py with re-diagnosis loop in cli.py after Globalping merge. No security issues or convention violations found.

events.summary: APPROVE — all 8 acceptance criteria pass, 141 tests pass, ruff clean. The implementation correctly eliminates the false High Jitter verdicts across all three phases: fallback probe count is now honored and capped at 5 with a scaled timeout, the verdict engine suppresses the High Jitter check when fewer than 5 samples exist, the mtr→paris→traceroute chain falls through silently when probers lack raw-socket access, and country sweeps re-derive verdicts from near-target Globalping figures after the merge so the summary table and exit code reflect ISP quality rather than the long-haul local trace. Ready to advance to the release stage.

workflow.rationale: Fixes existing broken behavior (false High Jitter verdicts) with no UI beyond existing terminal tables — skip design. Work follows established single-purpose module conventions (new paris.py mirrors pmtu.py/latency.py, diagnosis gating mirrors the rate-limited-hop pattern) and touches ~6 files across trace, diagnosis, and remote-measurement layers without new system boundaries — skip architecture; detailed phases with Files and Verification carry the design.

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Meaningful samples in the fallback prober

**What was built:**

- `src/netpath/mtr.py` — `_run_traceroute_cmd` gains a `probes` parameter (default 2, matching the old hardcoded behavior) with a public `TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES = 5` cap; the effective count is clamped to 1–5 and the subprocess timeout scales with it (`30 * effective + 15` seconds, so probes=2 stays near the old 60 s while probes=5 gets 165 s headroom for a slow filtered path). `run_traceroute` now threads its `probes` parameter into all four `_run_traceroute_cmd` call sites (TCP-first, UDP fallback, UDP-first, TCP retry).
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — after the trace section in `_measure()`, when `_trace_method` is `"traceroute"` the result's `probe_count` is corrected from `cycles` to the effective fallback count `min(cycles, mtr.TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES)`, covering all three trace branches (dual-stack, ECMP, plain).
- `src/netpath/diagnosis.py` — new `JITTER_MIN_SAMPLES = 5` constant; check (5) now emits an ok-severity `jitter_low_sample` informational signal instead of the High Jitter warning when `probe_count` is present and below 5. `probe_count=None` (unknown) keeps legacy warning behavior. `jitter_low_sample` maps to "Healthy" in the condition-verdict table, mirroring the rate-limited-hop pattern.

**Tests:** 3 new cases in `tests/test_mtr.py` (probe threading via mocked `subprocess.run`, cap at 5, timeout scaling) and 3 in `tests/test_diagnosis.py` (suppression at probe_count=2, warning at 5 and at 10). Two exact-call assertions in `tests/test_country.py` updated for the new `probes` kwarg. Suite: 109 passed, ruff clean.

**Deviations from plan:** none.

## Phase 2: Consistent-route probing via a Paris-capable prober

**What was built:**

- `src/netpath/paris.py` (new) — single-purpose module for Paris-style route-pinned probing. `detect()` returns the first installed supported binary (`dublin-traceroute` preferred, then `scamper`) or None; `available()` wraps it. `run(host, probes, binary)` caps probes at `PARIS_MAX_PROBES = 5`, dispatches to the binary-specific runner, and raises `ParisError` on any failure — non-zero exit (covers raw-socket permission denial), timeout, unreadable or empty output, missing binary — so callers can fall through silently. Successful runs get Cymru name enrichment via the shared `mtr._enrich_names`.
  - dublin-traceroute runner: invokes the binary once per probe with `--npaths=1` and a temp-dir `--output-file`, so every sample per TTL rides the same pinned flow (aggregating a single multi-path invocation would reintroduce ECMP diversity); `_parse_dublin_outputs` aggregates the runs per TTL into `Hub`-shaped dicts.
  - scamper runner: one invocation of `scamper -O json -I "trace -P icmp-paris -q {probes} -Q -w 1 -m 30 {host}"`; `_parse_scamper_output` parses the line-delimited JSON, groups replies by `probe_ttl`, computes fractional loss from `attempts`, and fills unanswered TTLs up to `hop_count` as `???` hops.
  - Both parsers are pure module-private functions producing hop dicts with `count`, `host`, `ASN`, `Loss%`, `Avg`, `Best`, `Wrst`, `StDev`, `p50`, `p95`, `p99` — matching the `Hub` TypedDict and the existing traceroute parser's conventions.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — new `_fallback_trace()` helper implements the mtr → paris → traceroute chain; `_trace()` and the ECMP branch's `MtrPermissionError` handler both use it. `_trace_method` carries the Paris binary name, so `_measure()` corrects `probe_count` to `min(cycles, paris.PARIS_MAX_PROBES)` for Paris-sourced traces, and `_run_test()` prints "(mtr unavailable — using {binary} Paris traceroute + Cymru ASN lookup)" naming the prober.

**Tests:** 20 new cases in `tests/test_paris.py`: detection order, dublin parser (aggregation, fractional loss, unresponsive hops, empty payload), scamper parser (grouping, gap fill, fractional loss, missing trace object), `run()` (no binary, probe cap, Paris command construction, permission and OS errors mapped to `ParisError`), and the `cli._trace` fallback chain (Paris selected on mtr permission error, traceroute when Paris absent or failing, mtr untouched when it works). Suite: 129 passed, ruff clean.

**Deviations from plan:** none.

## Phase 3: Near-target measurements drive country-sweep verdicts

**What was built:**

- `src/netpath/globalping.py` — ping scheduling now requests `_PING_PACKETS = 16` packets (the API per-measurement maximum) instead of 3. New pure parser `parse_ping_stats(results)` returns `{"loss_pct", "jitter_ms", "packets"}`: loss aggregates summed `drop`/`total` counts across probes, jitter is the per-probe StDev of its packet `timings` aggregated across probes via the median (robust to one bad probe), and `packets` is the count of received timings the jitter rests on. Returns None when no probe carried usable data; each key is present only when computable.
- `src/netpath/diagnosis.py` — new `REMOTE_MIN_PACKETS = 5` constant. `diagnose()` reads the per-row `globalping` dict defensively (missing, None, non-dict, or partial all degrade to local behavior). When a valid remote sample exists (`ping_packets >= 5`) and `ping_jitter_ms` is present, the remote figure drives check (5): above 10 ms it fires `high_jitter` citing the near-target value; otherwise a local jitter that would have warned is suppressed with an ok-severity `jitter_remote_clean` note naming the near-target source. A new check (5b) fires a `remote_packet_loss` warning (verdict "Near-target Packet Loss") when `ping_loss_pct` exceeds the existing calibrated loss tiers applied to the remote packet count — independent of the jitter suppression, so clean remote jitter cannot hide genuine near-target loss.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — the Globalping merge fetches ping results once and feeds both `parse_ping_rtt` and `parse_ping_stats`, storing `ping_loss_pct`, `ping_jitter_ms`, and `ping_packets` in the per-row `globalping` dict. Before `display.country_summary()` and the exit-code computation, a re-diagnosis loop re-runs `diagnose(row)` for rows that have a verdict and gained at least one remote figure — skipped ASNs (no `verdict` key) and uncovered ASNs are untouched, preserving the existing exit-code contract.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — the Globalping subrow in the country summary now shows `loss X%` and `jitter Y ms` next to the RTT, and appends `verdict {V} (near-target)` when remote figures exist, so the authoritative post-merge verdict and its source are visible where the per-ISP panels printed earlier may disagree.

**Tests:** 6 new cases in `tests/test_globalping.py` (16-packet payload, stats extraction, median jitter aggregation, cross-probe loss aggregation, received-timing count, None on unusable data) and 6 in `tests/test_diagnosis.py` (clean-remote suppression with the near-target note, remote-jitter warning citing the remote figure, near-target loss warning, suppression/loss independence, below-minimum remote sample falls back to local, absent/malformed remote data keeps local behavior). Suite: 141 passed, ruff clean.

**Deviations from plan:** none.
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 11 files changed across 3 phases

All 8 acceptance criteria pass. The three-phase fix correctly addresses the false High Jitter verdict root causes: probe count is honored and capped, the verdict engine gates on sample size, the mtr→paris→traceroute chain falls through silently, and country sweeps re-derive verdicts from near-target Globalping figures. 141 tests pass, ruff clean.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite (pytest) | PASS | 141 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped |
| Lint (ruff) | PASS | All checks passed |

### Acceptance Criteria (8/8 passed)

- [x] AC-1: The fallback prober sends the requested per-hop probe count capped at 5; verified by tests/test_mtr.py cases asserting the constructed traceroute command for probes=3 and probes=10 via a mocked subprocess.run. — PASS: mtr.py:198 TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES=5; mtr.py:208 effective_probes = max(1, min(probes, TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES)); tests/test_mtr.py:153-164 assert -q 3 for probes=3 and -q 5 for probes=10.
- [x] AC-2: A jitter figure derived from fewer than 5 samples never produces a High Jitter warning and yields an informational signal instead; verified by tests/test_diagnosis.py cases with probe_count of 2 and of 5. — PASS: diagnosis.py:2 JITTER_MIN_SAMPLES=5; diagnosis.py:230 gate probe_count < JITTER_MIN_SAMPLES -> jitter_low_sample ok-severity; tests/test_diagnosis.py:91-111 probe_count=2 yields jitter_low_sample Healthy, probe_count=5 yields High Jitter warning.
- [x] AC-3: The Paris module parses canned prober output into hop dicts carrying hop number, host, loss, and RTT statistics; verified by pure-function tests in tests/test_paris.py. — PASS: paris.py:66-92 _parse_dublin_outputs; paris.py:95-138 _parse_scamper_output; both produce Hub-shaped dicts with count, host, Loss%, Avg, Best, Wrst, StDev, p50, p95, p99. tests/test_paris.py:77-141 assert all fields including fractional loss and unresponsive hops.
- [x] AC-4: With mtr permission-denied and a Paris binary present the trace uses the Paris prober, and with no Paris binary it uses the system traceroute; verified by tests mocking binary detection and subprocess.run. — PASS: cli.py:155-163 _fallback_trace() tries paris.run then falls through to mtr.run_traceroute; cli.py:166-170 _trace() handles MtrPermissionError via _fallback_trace. tests/test_paris.py:206-242 all three chain paths verified.
- [x] AC-5: Remote ping scheduling requests 16 packets and the new parser returns loss percentage and jitter from a canned Globalping results payload; verified by tests in tests/test_globalping.py. — PASS: globalping.py:16 _PING_PACKETS=16; globalping.py:96 measurementOptions={packets:16}; globalping.py:200-242 parse_ping_stats returns loss_pct, jitter_ms, packets. tests/test_globalping.py:109-117 asserts payload, 235-281 cover all parser paths.
- [x] AC-6: diagnose() with a 20 ms local-trace jitter and clean near-target figures returns no High Jitter warning and includes an informational signal naming the near-target source; with near-target jitter above 10 ms it returns a High Jitter warning citing the near-target figure; verified in tests/test_diagnosis.py. — PASS: diagnosis.py:208-228: remote_valid and remote_jitter <= threshold -> jitter_remote_clean ok-severity with 'near-target' in detail; remote_jitter > threshold -> high_jitter warning citing remote value. tests/test_diagnosis.py:114-137 both cases verified.
- [x] AC-7: diagnose() with no near-target data present behaves identically to the phase 1 behavior for the same inputs; verified by regression cases in tests/test_diagnosis.py. — PASS: diagnosis.py:53-65 gp read defensively; non-dict / None / missing / partial all leave remote_valid=False. tests/test_diagnosis.py:164-181 packets=3 and absent/malformed cases all yield High Jitter verdict matching phase 1 behavior.
- [x] AC-8: pytest and ruff check src tests pass with no regressions in the existing suite. — PASS: pytest: 141 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. ruff check src tests: All checks passed.

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- All three levers from the brief are in scope as separate phases: raise the fallback sample count, add best-effort Paris-capable prober support, and gate country-sweep verdicts on Globalping near-target loss/jitter.
- Remote-figure verdict gating lives inside the pure diagnose() function via new keys on the result dict; country mode re-runs diagnose() on rows that gained remote data after the Globalping merge, before the summary table and exit-code computation.
- diagnose() receives arbitrary dicts where probe_count may be absent; treating None as unknown rather than too few preserves every existing test and the mtr path (cycles=10) unchanged.
- dublin-traceroute is invoked once per probe with --npaths=1 and the runs aggregated per TTL; _trace_method carries the Paris binary name rather than a generic paris value.
- The minimum-sample gate for near-target figures lives in diagnose() as REMOTE_MIN_PACKETS=5, keyed on ping_packets (count of received timings); the near-target loss check reuses the existing calibrated loss-threshold tiers applied to the remote packet count.

## Required Changes

None.

reviewPayload.summary: All 8 acceptance criteria pass. The three-phase fix correctly addresses the false High Jitter verdict root causes: probe count is honored and capped, the verdict engine gates on sample size, the mtr→paris→traceroute chain falls through silently, and country sweeps re-derive verdicts from near-target Globalping figures. 141 tests pass, ruff clean.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The fallback prober sends the requested per-hop probe count capped at 5; verified by tests/test_mtr.py cases asserting the constructed traceroute command for probes=3 and probes=10 via a mocked subprocess.run.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: mtr.py:198 TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES=5; mtr.py:208 effective_probes = max(1, min(probes, TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES)); tests/test_mtr.py:153-164 assert -q 3 for probes=3 and -q 5 for probes=10.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A jitter figure derived from fewer than 5 samples never produces a High Jitter warning and yields an informational signal instead; verified by tests/test_diagnosis.py cases with probe_count of 2 and of 5.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: diagnosis.py:2 JITTER_MIN_SAMPLES=5; diagnosis.py:230 gate probe_count < JITTER_MIN_SAMPLES -> jitter_low_sample ok-severity; tests/test_diagnosis.py:91-111 probe_count=2 yields jitter_low_sample Healthy, probe_count=5 yields High Jitter warning.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The Paris module parses canned prober output into hop dicts carrying hop number, host, loss, and RTT statistics; verified by pure-function tests in tests/test_paris.py.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: paris.py:66-92 _parse_dublin_outputs; paris.py:95-138 _parse_scamper_output; both produce Hub-shaped dicts with count, host, Loss%, Avg, Best, Wrst, StDev, p50, p95, p99. tests/test_paris.py:77-141 assert all fields including fractional loss and unresponsive hops.

acceptanceCriteria.text: With mtr permission-denied and a Paris binary present the trace uses the Paris prober, and with no Paris binary it uses the system traceroute; verified by tests mocking binary detection and subprocess.run.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:155-163 _fallback_trace() tries paris.run then falls through to mtr.run_traceroute; cli.py:166-170 _trace() handles MtrPermissionError via _fallback_trace. tests/test_paris.py:206-242 all three chain paths verified.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Remote ping scheduling requests 16 packets and the new parser returns loss percentage and jitter from a canned Globalping results payload; verified by tests in tests/test_globalping.py.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: globalping.py:16 _PING_PACKETS=16; globalping.py:96 measurementOptions={packets:16}; globalping.py:200-242 parse_ping_stats returns loss_pct, jitter_ms, packets. tests/test_globalping.py:109-117 asserts payload, 235-281 cover all parser paths.

acceptanceCriteria.text: diagnose() with a 20 ms local-trace jitter and clean near-target figures returns no High Jitter warning and includes an informational signal naming the near-target source; with near-target jitter above 10 ms it returns a High Jitter warning citing the near-target figure; verified in tests/test_diagnosis.py.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: diagnosis.py:208-228: remote_valid and remote_jitter <= threshold -> jitter_remote_clean ok-severity with 'near-target' in detail; remote_jitter > threshold -> high_jitter warning citing remote value. tests/test_diagnosis.py:114-137 both cases verified.

acceptanceCriteria.text: diagnose() with no near-target data present behaves identically to the phase 1 behavior for the same inputs; verified by regression cases in tests/test_diagnosis.py.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: diagnosis.py:53-65 gp read defensively; non-dict / None / missing / partial all leave remote_valid=False. tests/test_diagnosis.py:164-181 packets=3 and absent/malformed cases all yield High Jitter verdict matching phase 1 behavior.

acceptanceCriteria.text: pytest and ruff check src tests pass with no regressions in the existing suite.
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: pytest: 141 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. ruff check src tests: All checks passed.

decisions: All three levers from the brief are in scope as separate phases: raise the fallback sample count, add best-effort Paris-capable prober support, and gate country-sweep verdicts on Globalping near-target loss/jitter.

Remote-figure verdict gating lives inside the pure diagnose() function via new keys on the result dict; country mode re-runs diagnose() on rows that gained remote data after the Globalping merge, before the summary table and exit-code computation.
diagnose() receives arbitrary dicts where probe_count may be absent; treating None as unknown rather than too few preserves every existing test and the mtr path (cycles=10) unchanged.
dublin-traceroute is invoked once per probe with --npaths=1 and the runs aggregated per TTL; _trace_method carries the Paris binary name rather than a generic paris value.

The minimum-sample gate for near-target figures lives in diagnose() as REMOTE_MIN_PACKETS=5, keyed on ping_packets (count of received timings); the near-target loss check reuses the existing calibrated loss-threshold tiers applied to the remote packet count.

changeAnnotations.note: Adds TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES=5 and threads the probes parameter through _run_traceroute_cmd with a scaled subprocess timeout.

Wires mtr->paris->traceroute fallback chain, corrects probe_count for Paris-sourced traces, merges parse_ping_stats figures into globalping dicts, and re-derives verdicts before the country summary and exit code.

Adds sample-size gate on High Jitter (jitter_low_sample), near-target jitter suppression/warning (jitter_remote_clean / high_jitter via remote), and independent near-target loss check (remote_packet_loss).
Extends the Globalping subrow to show loss% and jitter ms, and appends the post-merge verdict with a (near-target) label.
Raises ping packet count to 16 (_PING_PACKETS) and adds parse_ping_stats() to extract loss_pct, jitter_ms, and received packet count.
New module: best-effort Paris traceroute via dublin-traceroute or scamper with silent ParisError fall-through on any failure.
Three new cases verify probe count threading, cap at 5, and timeout scaling.
New test file: 20 cases covering detection, both parsers, run() failure mapping, and the cli fallback chain.
Nine new cases for jitter suppression, remote-figure gating, near-target loss, and malformed-remote-data regression.
Six new cases verifying the 16-packet payload, parse_ping_stats extraction paths, and None on unusable data.
Two existing assertions updated for the new probes kwarg added to run_traceroute.
